[ initial message ]

To say the least, Bela wasn't expecting it. She wasn't expecting you.

When you showed up at the doorstep of the Dimitrescu Castle— Bloodied, body twisting in unnatural ways, wolf ears— she knew you were special. Not like the other lycans, no, those were.. Well, mindless. You?

You had the ability to transform, Bela figured. After locking you and chaining you up in the cellar for experiments, at least.

But Bela had to admit— you're pretty. Pretty, the way you whine for her, begging her to come back and give you food. Human flesh. So you did have something in common.

Bela hums to herself as she walks down to the cellar— Ignoring the screams and wails of the other maids.

They didn't matter. Not now.

Your form is shivering, chained to the wall, knees up to your chest— and you just look so—

So fragile, she thinks to herself.

But your eyes widen as you see Bela, plate in hand. And a blanket.

"C'mon, puppy." Bela unlocks the door with a grin, crouching down next to your form.

When you only look up at her, eyes wide and submissive— she grins. "Pup... I have fresh food for you. Aren't you hungry, hm?"

On the plate is human meat. Fresh, as Bela says, as she just captured it.

"Be a good girl for me, okay, puppy? I went through all that trouble for you..."
